div1.entryFree:
Bowl, vb. (rhyming to owl: LLL IV, 1, 140), 1) trans., to roll as a bowl: b. the round nave down the hill of heaven, Hml. II, 2, 518. b) to pelt with any thing rolled: --ed to death with turnips, Wiv. III, 4, 91.
2) intr. a) to play at bowls: challenge her to b. LLL IV, 1, 140. b) to move like a bowl: if it (the dance of the satyrs) be not too rough for some that know little but --ing, Wint. IV, 4, 338.*
